novelty_rationale: The transmission of institutional investor liability structure into commercial property lease pricing represents an unexplored channel connecting pension fund regulation to real estate asset mispricing.
nn_title: Real Estate in an ALM Framework: The Case of Fair Value Accounting
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: Does air quality improvement from the Clean Air Act's National Ambient Air Quality Standards attainment designation causally increase residential land values and stimulate new housing construction?
method: Regression discontinuity design exploiting the discrete attainment/nonattainment threshold for PM2.5 and ozone concentrations, measuring capitalization effects on residential transaction prices and building permit volumes.
data: EPA ambient air quality monitoring data merged with CoreLogic residential transaction data, Census Building Permits Survey, and county-level demographic controls covering 1990-2020 across U.S. counties.
key_finding: Counties achieving Clean Air Act attainment designation are expected to experience 3-8% higher residential land values and 5-15% increases in new construction permits relative to just-nonattaining counties.
novelty_rationale: While air pollution's hedonic price effects are established, the causal effect of regulatory attainment status—as a discrete policy treatment—on both housing prices and new supply has not been identified using the regulatory threshold discontinuity.
nn_title: Does air pollution affect investor cognition and land valuation? Evidence from t
assigned_label: Environmental hazards and property valuation

research_question: How does the geographic allocation of commercial real estate private debt capital by non-bank lenders affect local credit availability, property price cycles, and the risk of commercial mortgage defaults?
method: Panel analysis using Trepp CMBS loan-level data combined with debt fund origination records from Preqin to construct metropolitan-level measures of non-bank CRE debt penetration, with instrumental variables using state banking regulation as instruments.
data: Trepp CMBS loan performance database merged with Preqin private debt fund transaction data, MSCI/IPD commercial price indices, and Federal Reserve Senior Loan Officer Survey data covering 2010-2023.
key_finding: Higher non-bank CRE debt penetration is expected to amplify local commercial property price cycles by 20-40% and increase loan default rates during downturns, as non-banks face fewer regulatory constraints on lending standards.
novelty_rationale: The dramatic rise of non-bank commercial real estate lenders post-2010 has fundamentally altered credit markets, yet the systemic consequences for property price cycles and mortgage risk remain empirically uncharacterized.
nn_title: Regional Redistribution through the US Mortgage Market
assigned_label: Regional variation in mortgage market structure

research_question: What is the causal effect of transit-oriented affordable housing developments on neighborhood income diversity, school quality capitalization, and long-run resident economic mobility?
method: Difference-in-differences using the quasi-random timing of transit-oriented affordable housing project approvals relative to school boundary changes, with long-run follow-up on resident earnings using matched tax records.
data: HUD LIHTC property location database merged with Census LEHD employer-employee records, GreatSchools quality ratings, Opportunity Insights neighborhood mobility estimates, and transit agency GIS data covering 1995-2020.
key_finding: Children raised within a quarter-mile of transit-oriented affordable housing are expected to show 8-15% higher young-adult earnings, with effects concentrated in developments near high-quality school attendance zones.
novelty_rationale: The long-run intergenerational economic mobility consequences of affordable housing location relative to transit access and school quality have not been causally identified despite enormous policy relevance.
nn_title: School finance, spatial income segregation, and the nature of communities
assigned_label: School quality capitalization into housing prices

research_question: How does the spatial pattern of zoning reform—specifically, upzoning along transit corridors versus citywide—affect the magnitude and distribution of housing supply responses and spillover effects on adjacent land values?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences comparing corridor-only upzoning (e.g., Minneapolis, Seattle, Portland) against citywide reform, with parcel-level building permit and transaction price data to measure supply and price spillovers.
data: Parcel-level zoning change records from municipal GIS databases merged with Census Building Permits Survey, CoStar multifamily development data, and CoreLogic transaction prices covering 2015-2023 in reform cities.
key_finding: Corridor upzoning is expected to concentrate new supply within a quarter-mile of transit lines with minimal citywide impact, while citywide reform produces 30-60% more distributed supply growth with smaller localized price effects.
novelty_rationale: Recent zoning reforms across U.S. cities provide a natural experiment to compare the supply and price consequences of targeted versus comprehensive upzoning, a distinction ignored in prior research that treats reform as a binary treatment.
nn_title: The capitalization of metro rail access in urban housing markets
assigned_label: Land use regulation and property values

research_question: Do REIT managers with prior private equity real estate experience generate superior risk-adjusted returns through better property selection, and does this human capital premium vary with portfolio transparency?
method: Cross-sectional and panel regression analysis linking biographical executive background data to REIT property-level acquisition premiums, NOI growth, and stock-based performance metrics, controlling for firm size and property type.
data: S&P Capital IQ and Bloomberg executive biography databases merged with CRSP-Compustat REIT financial data, MSCI/NCREIF property transaction records, and SNL Real Estate executive compensation data covering 1995-2022.
key_finding: REIT managers with prior PERE experience are expected to generate 50-150 basis points higher annual risk-adjusted returns, with effects strongest in opaque property types where private market knowledge is most valuable.
novelty_rationale: While CEO characteristics affect corporate outcomes broadly, the specific value of private real estate market experience for public REIT managers—who must navigate both private property markets and public capital markets—has not been systematically examined.
nn_title: Privately Versus Publicly Held Asset Investment Performance
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: How does the geographic expansion of fiber broadband infrastructure causally affect rural and suburban housing demand, prices, and the composition of in-migrants from urban areas?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting FCC broadband deployment subsidies under the Connect America Fund as quasi-random assignment of high-speed internet access, with heterogeneous effects estimated by pre-pandemic urban proximity.
data: FCC Form 477 broadband availability data merged with FCC CAF subsidy award records, Zillow transaction-level price data, IRS Statistics of Income migration flow data, and Census American Community Survey covering 2010-2023.
key_finding: Fiber broadband deployment is expected to raise rural and exurban housing prices by 5-15% and increase in-migration of high-income remote workers, with effects intensifying after 2020 as remote work became normalized.
novelty_rationale: While COVID-era urban-to-suburban migration has been documented, the role of broadband infrastructure as a durable driver of residential location sorting and rural housing market development has not been causally identified.
nn_title: The remote work revolution: Impact on real estate values and the urban environme
assigned_label: Residential mobility and migration determinants

research_question: How does the legal framework governing residential lease termination affect long-run rental housing supply, renter mobility, and the cross-sectional distribution of rents?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting variation in state-level just-cause eviction law adoption across U.S. states, measuring effects on multifamily housing permits, vacancy rates, rent levels, and tenant mobility rates.
data: NMHC and Urban Institute just-cause eviction law database merged with Census American Housing Survey, CoStar multifamily rent and vacancy data, Princeton Eviction Lab, and Census Building Permits Survey covering 1990-2022.
key_finding: Just-cause eviction laws are expected to reduce multifamily housing supply by 5-15% over a decade while decreasing evictions by 20-35%, with net rent effects of 3-8% increases as reduced supply offsets the direct tenant protection benefit.
novelty_rationale: While rent control has been extensively studied, the supply and market-level consequences of just-cause eviction protections—which are expanding rapidly across U.S. jurisdictions—have received almost no causal empirical analysis.
nn_title: The Effects of Rent Control Expansion on Tenants, Landlords, and Inequality: Evi
assigned_label: Rental Markets, Policy Effects, and Housing Supply

research_question: Does the concentration of single-family rental institutional ownership in a metropolitan area affect local labor market mobility, specifically by reducing worker willingness to relocate for job opportunities?
method: Instrumental variables using pension fund SFR investment flows driven by institutional home-state bias as instruments for local SFR penetration, measuring effects on county-to-county migration flows for job-related moves from IRS data.
data: IRS Statistics of Income county-to-county migration data merged with Invitation Homes, AMH, and Progress Residential SEC filings for SFR ownership concentration, BLS local area unemployment statistics, and JOLTS job openings data covering 2012-2022.
key_finding: A 10 percentage point increase in institutional SFR market share is expected to reduce job-related out-migration by 3-7% by making flexible renting less feasible and effectively trapping residents in housing markets with limited supply.
novelty_rationale: The homeownership-unemployment lock-in mechanism is well-documented, but whether institutional single-family rental concentration creates a parallel rental-sector lock-in through market power and lease inflexibility has not been examined.
nn_title: Residential Choice, Mobility, and the Labor Market
assigned_label: Household residential location and mobility decisions

research_question: How does the presence of active options markets for individual REITs affect the accuracy and speed of price discovery for their underlying commercial property assets in private markets?
method: Panel analysis using the staggered listing of REIT options on major exchanges as a quasi-natural experiment, measuring effects on the lead-lag relationship between REIT stock prices and matched NCREIF private property appraisals.
data: OptionMetrics REIT options data merged with CRSP-Compustat daily REIT stock prices, NCREIF property-level appraisal data matched by property type and geography, and CoStar transaction records covering 2000-2023.
key_finding: REIT option listing is expected to accelerate private market price discovery by 1-3 quarters, reducing the autocorrelation in matched NCREIF appraisal returns by 20-40% as private valuers extract signals from more information-rich public markets.
novelty_rationale: The established finding that REIT returns lead private market returns suggests information flows from public to private real estate, but the specific role of derivatives markets in accelerating this channel has not been investigated.
nn_title: Returns and Information Transmission Dynamics in Public and Private Real Estate
assigned_label: Real Estate Asset Valuation and Returns

research_question: What is the causal effect of neighborhood-level gang territory boundaries on residential property values, housing investment, and the spatial distribution of credit access?
method: Regression discontinuity design exploiting sharp gang territory boundaries in Chicago and Los Angeles as identified by law enforcement gang audit data, measuring discontinuities in property prices, building permits, and mortgage originations.
data: Chicago and Los Angeles Police Department gang boundary audit data merged with CoreLogic residential transaction data, Census Building Permits Survey, HMDA origination records, and business license data covering 2000-2022.
key_finding: Properties immediately inside gang territory boundaries are expected to sell at 8-18% discounts relative to comparable properties just outside boundaries, with discontinuities in mortgage denial rates of 15-30 percentage points.
novelty_rationale: While crime generally affects property values, the use of gang territory boundaries as a quasi-experimental design to identify the local capitalization of criminal territorial control represents a methodologically novel approach with direct policy implications.
nn_title: Gated Communities and the Economic Geography of Crime
assigned_label: Crime's economic effects on property and wages

research_question: How does the depletion of groundwater aquifers in agricultural regions affect farmland values, rural residential property prices, and the long-run viability of local mortgage markets?
method: Difference-in-differences using satellite-derived GRACE groundwater depletion data as a continuous treatment measure, with event studies around well permit moratoriums as discrete shocks to water availability.
data: NASA GRACE satellite groundwater anomaly data merged with USDA land value surveys, CoreLogic rural residential transaction data, HMDA rural mortgage origination data, and USGS well permit records covering 2003-2022.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation decrease in aquifer storage is expected to reduce agricultural land values by 10-20% and rural residential prices by 5-10%, with mortgage denial rates rising 20-35% as lenders price in long-run habitability risk.
novelty_rationale: Water scarcity represents a slow-moving but existential risk to real estate in arid regions; the causal relationship between aquifer depletion and property markets has not been empirically identified despite growing policy urgency.
nn_title: The value of scarce water: Measuring the inefficiency of municipal regulations
assigned_label: Environmental hazards and property valuation

research_question: Does the co-location of universities and hospitals in innovation districts generate nonlinear agglomeration externalities for surrounding commercial real estate that exceed the sum of individual anchor effects?
method: Difference-in-differences with interaction terms comparing commercial real estate rent and vacancy trajectories around innovation districts with both anchors versus areas with only one anchor type, using propensity-score matched controls.
data: Brookings Institution innovation district mapping merged with CoStar commercial rent and vacancy data, NSF HERD university R&D expenditure data, CMS hospital characteristics data, and Census LEHD employment data covering 2000-2022.
key_finding: Innovation districts with both university and hospital anchors are expected to show 15-30% higher commercial rent premiums than would be predicted by additive effects alone, consistent with cross-sector knowledge spillovers generating superadditive agglomeration.
novelty_rationale: The nonlinear interaction effects between different types of knowledge-intensive anchor institutions on surrounding commercial property values have not been isolated from compositional and selection effects despite policy interest in innovation districts.
nn_title: Agglomeration elasticities and firm heterogeneity
assigned_label: Spatial Economics and Urban Land Values

research_question: How do climate migration flows from high-risk coastal and heat-stressed regions affect housing prices, rental affordability, and neighborhood composition in receiving metropolitan areas?
method: Instrumental variables using differential exposure to NOAA-documented extreme heat and sea-level rise projections as instruments for climate-related out-migration, measuring effects on housing prices and demographic composition in destination metros.
data: IRS Statistics of Income county migration flow data merged with NOAA sea-level rise and heat index data, Zillow transaction and rental price data, American Community Survey demographic data, and FEMA flood risk scores covering 2010-2023.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation increase in climate-driven in-migration is expected to raise housing prices in receiving metros by 3-8% and increase rent burdens for existing low-income residents by 5-12%, with effects concentrated in affordable inland metros.
novelty_rationale: While internal climate migration is theoretically predicted, its causal effects on housing markets in destination cities—a second-order climate risk that has received virtually no empirical real estate research—represent a critical and policy-relevant gap.
nn_title: When climate meets real estate: A survey of the literature
assigned_label: Natural disasters and real estate market dynamics

research_question: Does large-language-model sentiment extracted from earnings call transcripts, social media, and news predict commercial real estate transaction prices and cap rate movements beyond traditional fundamentals?
method: Train domain-adapted transformer models (fine-tuned BERT/GPT variants) on a novel corpus of REIT earnings calls, CoStar deal narratives, and commercial real estate news to construct daily sentiment scores, then use panel instrumental-variable regressions and Granger-causality tests to identify predictive content for transaction prices and cap rates net of macro controls.
data: REIT earnings call transcripts (2000–2024) from Refinitiv, CoStar transaction database, MSCI/IPD appraisal data, and Bloomberg news archives covering 25+ U.S. metro office/industrial markets.
key_finding: LLM-derived forward-looking uncertainty signals are likely to predict cap rate movements 2–4 quarters ahead with economically significant out-of-sample R², while standard sentiment indices add little marginal information, suggesting that semantic specificity—not mere polarity—drives pricing.
novelty_rationale: Prior corpus papers use simple news counts or Google Trends; importing domain-adapted transformer NLP into transaction-level commercial real estate pricing represents a genuinely new methodological frontier absent from both REE and JREFE.
nn_title: On the Relationship between Market Sentiment and Commercial Real Estate Performa
assigned_label: Real Estate Market Liquidity and Efficiency

research_question: How does the spatial diffusion of remote-work adoption across firms and households causally reshape the within-city rent gradient and the price premium attached to central business district proximity?
method: Exploit staggered, firm-level rollouts of permanent remote-work policies announced via 8-K filings and LinkedIn job postings as an instrumental variable for local telework intensity, then estimate a time-varying hedonic gradient model using census-tract-level repeat-sales and rental indices to recover the causal elasticity of CBD distance pricing with respect to commute-demand shocks.
data: Geocoded SEC 8-K announcements and LinkedIn job postings (2019–2024), Zillow/CoStar census-tract rental indices, CoreLogic repeat-sales transactions for 50 U.S. MSAs, and American Community Survey commuting data.
key_finding: A 10-percentage-point increase in firm-level remote-work adoption likely flattens the residential rent gradient by 4–8 percent and reallocates price premiums toward suburban nodes with green space and school quality, with effect magnitudes heterogeneous across pre-existing transit quality.
novelty_rationale: Existing corpus papers review remote-work impacts qualitatively or use aggregate COVID shocks; this paper introduces firm-level policy rollouts as an instrument to causally identify within-city gradient restructuring, opening a new empirical programme on technology-driven urban form.
nn_title: Commutes, Neighborhood Effects, and Compensating Differentials: Revisited
assigned_label: Commercial Office Market Dynamics and Pricing

research_question: Does algorithmic collusion among algorithmic pricing platforms (e.g., RealPage) used by large landlords cause supracompetitive rent increases, and can antitrust remedies restore competitive rent dynamics?
method: Use a staggered difference-in-differences design exploiting the sequential adoption of algorithmic revenue-management software across multifamily apartment markets, instrumenting adoption timing with plausibly exogenous software-vendor sales territory expansion, and compare rent levels, vacancy rates, and pricing dispersion in treated versus control submarkets.
data: CoStar/Yardi multifamily unit-level rent and vacancy panel (2010–2024), RealPage customer adoption disclosures from court filings, HUD fair-market rent data, and HMDA lending records for 40 MSAs.
key_finding: Coordinated algorithmic pricing adoption is likely to raise effective rents by 3–7 percent above competitive levels in concentrated markets, with spillover effects on owner-occupied housing prices, suggesting that software-mediated tacit collusion is a quantitatively important determinant of rental unaffordability.
novelty_rationale: No paper in the corpus examines algorithmic pricing platforms as a rent-setting mechanism; this imports the industrial-organization literature on algorithmic collusion into real estate economics, creating an entirely new research agenda on digital market power in housing.
nn_title: Do Wall Street Landlords Undermine Renters’ Welfare?
assigned_label: Rent Control Policy Effects and Outcomes

research_question: Can reinforcement-learning-based optimal stopping models outperform traditional rule-based and hazard-model approaches in predicting the timing of real estate development, mortgage prepayment, and property sales simultaneously within a unified framework?
method: Formulate the sequential decision of a real estate agent (develop, hold, sell, refinance, default) as a Markov decision process and train deep reinforcement learning agents (proximal policy optimization) on simulated and historical property-level microdata, then compare out-of-sample predictions of option-exercise timing against Cox hazard models and standard real-options closed-form solutions.
data: NCREIF property-level income and value histories (1978–2024), CoreLogic loan-level mortgage performance database, SNL Real Capital Analytics transaction records, and HMDA origination data covering 500,000+ asset histories.
key_finding: RL agents exploiting nonlinear state interactions (LTV, vacancy, local demand shocks, interest-rate regime) are likely to improve out-of-sample timing prediction by 15–25 percent relative to standard hazard models, particularly in high-volatility regimes where option-exercise boundary non-linearities are most severe.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains hedonic ML papers and real-options models but none applies reinforcement learning to endogenous real asset decision-making; this bridges computational finance and real estate economics in a way that could unify prepayment, development, and sale timing under a single learning framework.
nn_title: Deciphering the U.S. metropolitan house price dynamics
assigned_label: Machine Learning Automated Property Valuation Models

research_question: How do intergenerational wealth transfers and parental housing equity causally shape first-generation homeownership rates, housing quality choices, and long-run wealth accumulation, and does this mechanism explain a substantial share of the racial homeownership gap?
method: Link administrative estate and gift tax records to matched parent–child mortgage origination and property transaction data using a regression-discontinuity design around the estate-tax exemption threshold, combined with an event study of parental deaths as an exogenous shock to wealth transfers, to identify causal effects on child homeownership timing, location choice, and leverage.
data: IRS estate and gift tax microdata (linked via SSN to HMDA), CoreLogic deeds and mortgage records, American Community Survey, and Federal Reserve Survey of Consumer Finances (1989–2022) for a nationally representative panel of family dyads.
key_finding: Parental housing equity transfers likely account for 20–35 percent of the racial homeownership gap by enabling white households to enter ownership 3–5 years earlier and in higher-quality submarkets, with downstream effects on wealth accumulation that persist across two generations.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies parental wealth and coresidence separately but lacks causal identification of intergenerational equity transmission using administrative records; connecting estate tax discontinuities to housing market outcomes represents a novel methodological contribution that reframes the homeownership-gap literature.
nn_title: Mobility, Residential Location and the American Dream: The Intrametropolitan Geo
assigned_label: Racial disparities in homeownership and housing outcomes

research_question: Does the physical transition risk from decarbonization policy—specifically carbon pricing and building energy mandates—get differentially priced into commercial real estate assets, and do REITs with higher carbon-exposure portfolios face a systematic discount analogous to the oil-price beta in energy equities?
method: Construct a property-level carbon-transition exposure index using building energy benchmarking data and local climate policy stringency scores, then use a cross-sectional Fama-MacBeth asset-pricing framework to test whether this exposure commands a risk premium in REIT equity returns, with triple-difference event studies around major carbon-policy announcements.
data: Energy Star Portfolio Manager benchmarking disclosures (2010–2024), EPA ENERGY STAR scores, EU ETS auction prices, U.S. state-level building performance standard enactments, CoStar energy certificates for 80,000+ commercial properties, and CRSP/Compustat REIT equity returns.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation increase in portfolio carbon-transition exposure is likely associated with a 40–80 basis point annual return discount, with the premium intensifying after major regulatory announcements, suggesting that carbon risk is beginning to be priced systematically but remains partially unrecognized by markets.
novelty_rationale: Existing corpus papers examine green certification premiums but none constructs a carbon-transition risk factor for asset pricing of real estate; importing the carbon-risk literature from energy finance into a Fama-MacBeth REIT framework opens a new strand of climate–real estate finance research.
nn_title: Climate Change and Long-Run Discount Rates: Evidence from Real Estate
assigned_label: Natural Disasters, Climate Risk, and Real Estate Pricing

research_question: How do zoning reform shocks that upzone residential parcels affect incumbent homeowner wealth, renter welfare, neighborhood composition, and new housing supply elasticity across cities with heterogeneous land-use regulatory regimes?
method: Exploit parcel-level discontinuities in upzoning eligibility created by staggered zoning reform legislation across 15 U.S. and international cities (Minneapolis, Auckland, Oregon, Tokyo) using a geographic regression-discontinuity-in-differences design, estimating heterogeneous treatment effects on land values, new permits, existing home prices, rental rates, and neighborhood income sorting.
data: City parcel assessor records with zoning classifications (pre/post reform), building permit databases, Zillow/Redfin transaction-level prices, IPUMS American Community Survey, and New Zealand Territorial Authority permit databases (2016–2024).
key_finding: Upzoning raises land values for treated parcels by 15–30 percent while reducing nearby rental prices by 2–6 percent within 5 years as new supply materializes, but benefits are strongly conditioned on infrastructure capacity and school-zone quality, revealing a heterogeneous welfare incidence that challenges uniform deregulation prescriptions.
novelty_rationale: The corpus covers supply elasticity and zoning broadly, but the systematic multi-city parcel-level RD-in-differences design exploiting staggered upzoning reforms—with simultaneous welfare decomposition for owners, renters, and new entrants—represents a methodological and comparative advance beyond existing work.
nn_title: Intrajurisdictional Segmentation of Property Tax Burdens: Neighborhood Inequitie
assigned_label: Land use regulation and urban economic effects

research_question: Do mortgage servicers' algorithmic loan-modification decisions, which increasingly rely on black-box credit-scoring and expected-NPV models, produce racially disparate modification rates and loss outcomes compared to human-adjudicated modifications, and can causal audit methods identify discriminatory algorithmic channels?
method: Apply a correspondence audit design at scale by constructing synthetic loan portfolios that are identical across all risk-relevant covariates but vary only in demographic proxies (name, neighborhood racial composition, ZIP code), submit them to servicers' publicly documented automated NPV tools, and supplement with a regression-discontinuity analysis around documented NPV eligibility cutoffs in HAMP and proprietary servicer systems.
data: Black Knight McDash loan-level servicing database (2009–2020), HMDA modification disclosures, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau complaint records, and servicer NPV model documentation from regulatory filings for the 15 largest U.S. mortgage servicers.
key_finding: Algorithmic NPV tools likely produce modification approval rates 8–15 percentage points lower for minority borrowers at identical financial risk profiles, driven primarily by neighborhood property-value inputs that function as racial proxies, with downstream loss-given-default increases that harm both borrowers and investor pools.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains fair-lending and discrimination studies using observational data but none applies algorithmic audit methodology to mortgage modification decisions; importing the algorithmic-fairness auditing literature from computer science into mortgage economics creates an entirely new research strand.
nn_title: How Much Does Racial Bias Affect Mortgage Lending? Evidence from Human and Algor
assigned_label: Mortgage lending: discrimination, pricing, and risk

research_question: How does satellite-derived nighttime light intensity, land surface temperature, and rooftop solar capacity—observed at monthly cadence—predict REIT net operating income growth, property-level vacancy, and commercial real estate valuations in advance of traditional survey data?
method: Construct property-level physical activity indices from NASA Suomi NPP VIIRS nighttime lights, Landsat land surface temperature, and Google Solar API rooftop estimates matched to CoStar property identifiers, then use a panel nowcasting model (elastic net and gradient boosted trees) to predict NOI and cap rate changes 1–4 quarters ahead relative to NCREIF appraisal-based benchmarks.
data: NASA/NOAA VIIRS nighttime light rasters (2012–2024) at 500m resolution, Landsat-8/9 thermal imagery, CoStar property-level NOI and vacancy data, NCREIF NPI returns, and Google Building Footprint/Solar API for 200,000+ commercial properties across 30 MSAs.
key_finding: Satellite-derived economic activity indices likely predict NCREIF NOI changes with 12–20 percent lower forecast error than autoregressive benchmarks 2 quarters ahead, with the largest gains for retail and hospitality sectors where foot-traffic proxies are most informative, opening a new real-time data channel for commercial real estate investors.
novelty_rationale: Remote sensing and satellite imagery have transformed agricultural economics and macro forecasting but are entirely absent from both REE and JREFE; deploying physical-world observation satellites as a high-frequency nowcasting tool for commercial real estate fundamentals is methodologically unprecedented in this literature.
nn_title: Combining machine learning and econometrics: Application to commercial real esta
assigned_label: Real estate price index methodology and measurement

research_question: Does household exposure to extreme heat events—measured at the parcel level using high-resolution climate data—cause a revaluation of residential properties through a chronic risk-pricing mechanism distinct from the acute event-driven discounts documented in the natural-disaster literature, and does heat adaptation investment (cool roofs, HVAC upgrades) partially offset this discount?
method: Merge parcel-level repeat-sales transactions with 1-km gridded daily maximum wet-bulb temperature records (1980–2023) to estimate exposure-weighted chronic heat risk scores, use an instrumental-variables strategy leveraging pre-industrial urban heat island morphology as exogenous variation in chronic heat exposure, and exploit utility-rebate program rules as instruments for adaptation investment to identify the offset effect.
data: CoreLogic repeat-sales transactions (40 million+ observations), PRISM 800m gridded daily temperature and humidity data, EPA Energy Star residential renovation records, utility rebate program administrative data from California/Arizona/Texas, and county-level mortality records (1980–2023).
key_finding: Chronic heat exposure above 30 wet-bulb-degree-days per year likely reduces home values by 0.8–2.5 percent per additional degree-day, with the discount accelerating nonlinearly above thresholds associated with outdoor labor productivity loss, while certified cool-roof and high-efficiency HVAC upgrades offset 30–50 percent of this discount.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains flood and hurricane risk papers but none estimates the chronic heat risk gradient using high-resolution gridded climate data with parcel-matched repeat sales; the IV strategy exploiting pre-industrial UHI morphology and adaptation-offset identification represent methodological advances that connect climate science directly to housing economics.
nn_title: When climate meets real estate: A survey of the literature
assigned_label: Natural Disasters, Climate Risk, and Real Estate Pricing

research_question: Does the staggered rollout of high-speed internet infrastructure causally shift the spatial equilibrium of housing markets by reducing the premium placed on proximity to employment centers, and does this effect heterogeneously interact with pre-existing housing supply elasticity?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting the quasi-random timing of fiber-optic broadband rollout across census tracts, combined with a spatial equilibrium model that estimates simultaneous shifts in rent gradients, land prices, and residential location patterns.
data: FCC Form 477 broadband availability data matched to ZTRAX transaction records and LEHD commuting data covering U.S. census tracts from 2000–2022.
key_finding: High-speed internet access is expected to flatten rent gradients in elastic-supply metros by 8–15% while amplifying price appreciation in inelastic amenity-rich peripheries, with no significant effect in already-connected dense cores.
novelty_rationale: While remote work effects on housing are discussed in the corpus, no existing paper instruments for broadband infrastructure rollout to separately identify technology-driven demand decentralization from supply-side constraints within a full spatial equilibrium framework.
nn_title: U.S. metropolitan house price dynamics
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics, bubbles, and cycles

research_question: Do forensic accounting anomalies in REIT financial statements—specifically, patterns of accrual manipulation and non-GAAP earnings adjustments around FFO disclosures—predict future stock price crashes, and does auditor industry specialization moderate this relationship?
method: Beneish M-score decomposition applied to REIT-specific accrual structures combined with a crash risk model (NCSKEW and DUVOL measures) and auditor specialization proxies, estimated via panel regressions with REIT and year fixed effects.
data: COMPUSTAT/CRSP merged REIT panel from 1993–2022 linked to Audit Analytics for auditor identity and SNL Real Estate for FFO adjustments.
key_finding: REITs in the top quartile of accrual manipulation are expected to exhibit 20–35% higher crash risk probabilities in the following year, with specialist auditors reducing but not eliminating this relationship.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains work on REIT earnings management and crash risk separately but no study applies forensic accounting detection methods to REIT-specific FFO manipulation and links it causally to tail risk via auditor discipline.
nn_title: Manipulation in U.S. REIT Investment Performance Evaluation: Empirical Evidence
assigned_label: REIT Market Microstructure and Information

research_question: Can topological data analysis applied to the shape of rent gradient surfaces across metropolitan areas provide a novel early-warning signal for housing market instability that outperforms conventional price-to-rent ratio indicators?
method: Persistent homology applied to zip-code-level rent and price surfaces to extract topological features (Betti numbers, persistence diagrams) which are then used as predictors in quantile regression models for subsequent market downturns.
data: Zillow ZORI and ZHVI zip-code data from 2010–2023 across 100 U.S. metros, supplemented by CoreLogic transaction records for validation of predicted downturns.
key_finding: Topological complexity in rent surfaces, particularly the emergence of multiple disconnected high-rent clusters, is expected to precede price corrections by 6–12 months with out-of-sample AUC 10–15 percentage points above price-to-rent benchmarks.
novelty_rationale: Topological data analysis is entirely absent from the real estate economics corpus; importing this tool from computational mathematics creates a fundamentally new dimensionality-reduction approach to housing market surface characterization.
nn_title: Housing Price Cycle Interdependencies and Comovement: A Markov-Switching Approac
assigned_label: Housing market dynamics, bubbles, and cycles

research_question: How do peer-reviewed clinical epidemiological findings about neighborhood health risks—specifically, proximity to industrial pollution sources—get capitalized into commercial real estate lease spreads and cap rates, and does this process differ across tenant types with varying worker health sensitivity?
method: Event study methodology exploiting the staggered publication of Environmental Protection Agency toxic release inventory updates, combined with hedonic lease spread regressions that interact pollution exposure with tenant industry health sensitivity indices.
data: CoStar lease transaction database (2000–2022) matched to EPA Toxic Release Inventory facility-level data and BLS occupational injury rates by NAICS code for 50 major U.S. metros.
key_finding: Industrial pollution revelations are expected to increase cap rates by 15–30 basis points for affected office and industrial properties, with larger effects for health-care and food-processing tenants and negligible effects for logistics tenants.
novelty_rationale: The corpus links environmental hazards almost exclusively to residential prices; no study examines how occupational health externalities are priced into commercial lease contracts with heterogeneous tenant health sensitivity as the moderating mechanism.
nn_title: Commercial real estate and air pollution
assigned_label: Environmental Contamination and Property Values

research_question: Does the social capital of neighborhood religious institutions—measured by congregation size, denominational network breadth, and inter-faith diversity—causally affect residential mortgage default rates through informal credit networks and stigma suppression, independently of property value effects?
method: Instrumental variables estimation using historical church founding dates and denominational headquarters relocation events as instruments for current social capital, applied to loan-level mortgage performance data with neighborhood fixed effects.
data: HMDA loan-level performance data linked to IRS Form 990 religious organization filings and Pew Research Center denominational mapping covering U.S. census tracts from 2000–2019.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation increase in congregation-based social capital is expected to reduce 90-day delinquency rates by 3–7 percentage points, with stronger effects in lower-income neighborhoods where informal enforcement substitutes for formal contracting.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus contains work on social capital and mortgage delinquency, no study separately identifies the causal channel through religious institutional networks, instruments for endogenous social capital formation, or distinguishes informal credit norms from pure stigma suppression.
nn_title: Living next to godliness: Residential property values and churches
assigned_label: Mortgage lending: discrimination, pricing, and risk

research_question: Does the governance structure of condominium homeowners associations—specifically, the degree of democratic participation, reserve fund adequacy, and rules enforcement stringency—causally affect property value appreciation, maintenance quality, and neighbor externality internalization?
method: Regression discontinuity exploiting state-level thresholds in HOA reserve fund mandates, combined with hedonic price analysis using detailed HOA financial disclosure data and difference-in-differences around enforcement law changes.
data: Novel dataset linking county assessor transaction records to HOA financial disclosures from state regulatory filings in Florida, California, and Colorado (2005–2022), supplemented by condo association governance documents obtained via public records requests.
key_finding: Adequately funded HOAs subject to mandatory disclosure are expected to generate 4–9% price premiums over comparable under-funded associations, with reserve fund adequacy explaining more price variation than HOA fee levels alone.
novelty_rationale: The corpus is silent on internal governance mechanisms of HOAs as distinct from broader neighborhood institution effects; no study treats HOA reserve fund adequacy as an exogenous governance instrument or connects democratic participation rules to property value externality internalization.
nn_title: Land use regulations and the value of land and housing: An intra-metropolitan an
assigned_label: Real Estate Finance Research Frontiers

research_question: How do mortgage lenders' internal credit models, revealed through differences in their approval rates around FICO score discontinuities, generate systematic spatial misallocation of credit that amplifies neighborhood segregation and divergent house price trajectories?
method: Bunching estimation at FICO score cutoffs in HMDA data combined with spatial econometric analysis linking lender-specific approval discontinuities to 10-year neighborhood price and demographic convergence or divergence outcomes.
data: HMDA loan-level application and origination data (2000–2020) matched to FFIEC census tract demographics and Zillow neighborhood price indices, covering all federally regulated lenders.
key_finding: Neighborhoods located near lenders with sharp FICO-based approval discontinuities are expected to experience 15–25% greater racial income sorting over a decade, with divergent price trajectories attributable to credit rationing rather than fundamentals.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus covers racial discrimination in lending and neighborhood sorting separately, no paper uses bunching around lender-specific internal credit model discontinuities to causally link algorithmic credit allocation to multi-decade spatial segregation dynamics.
nn_title: Spatial Heterogeneity in Mortgage Terminations by Refinance, Sale and Default
assigned_label: Mortgage lending: discrimination, pricing, and risk

research_question: Do short-term rental platforms create informational spillovers that improve long-term rental market price discovery, or do they primarily extract information rents from tourists while degrading local renter welfare through increased price volatility and reduced housing supply?
method: Structural model of dual short-term/long-term rental market equilibrium estimated via simulated method of moments, calibrated using a natural experiment from Airbnb's platform entry across European cities at different regulatory regimes.
data: AirDNA short-term rental listings data matched to Eurostat regional long-term rent indices, housing stock records, and Airbnb regulatory timing from 50 European cities (2012–2022).
key_finding: Platform entry is expected to increase long-term rents by 3–8% on average while reducing rent volatility in tourist-heavy markets through improved spatial price signals, but harming renter welfare in supply-constrained housing markets through unit conversion.
novelty_rationale: While Airbnb ban effects on long-term rents appear in the corpus, no study models the dual information and supply channels simultaneously using structural estimation, nor separates informational externalities from physical housing conversion effects.
nn_title: Do short-term rental platforms affect housing markets? Evidence from Airbnb in B
assigned_label: Rent Control Policy Effects and Outcomes

research_question: Can network analysis of institutional investor co-ownership ties across commercial real estate portfolios identify systemically important nodes whose asset fire sales transmit price shocks across geographically dispersed markets, and does portfolio overlap predict contagion beyond financial leverage?
method: Construction of a bipartite network of institutional investors and property portfolios from NCREIF data, applying minimum spanning tree and ΔCoVaR systemic risk metrics to identify contagion pathways, validated against realized price dynamics during the 2008–2009 crisis.
data: NCREIF property-level ownership data (1995–2020) linked to CRSP/Compustat institutional holding files, CoStar transaction records, and RCA property distress flags for institutional sellers.
key_finding: A 10% increase in portfolio overlap between two institutional investors is expected to predict a 4–8 basis point correlation in quarterly price changes for their respective market holdings, with overlap-based contagion explaining 20–30% of cross-metro price comovement unexplained by macroeconomic fundamentals.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus contains granular risks and common ownership work for REITs, no study applies systemic risk network methodology (bipartite overlap + ΔCoVaR) to private institutional real estate portfolios to identify contagion pathways distinct from leverage transmission.
nn_title: Is there really any Contagion among Major Equity and Securitized Real Estate Mar
assigned_label: Real Estate Market Integration and Comovement Analysis

research_question: Does exposure to culturally distinct international diaspora communities within a neighborhood causally affect local housing demand patterns, price levels, and the valuation of architectural and amenity features in ways that persist beyond the initial settlement wave and reshape local hedonic price structures?
method: Shift-share instrumental variables exploiting the orthogonal allocation of diaspora groups across destinations combined with quantile hedonic regression to identify distributional shifts in implicit prices of housing features as cultural composition changes.
data: American Community Survey diaspora concentration data at the tract level matched to Zillow/CoreLogic transaction records with detailed structural characteristics (2000–2020) for 30 gateway metros, augmented with building permit data for new construction features.
key_finding: A 10 percentage point increase in co-ethnic concentration is expected to raise implicit prices for multi-generational floor plans by 8–14% and reduce implicit prices for single-car garages by 5–9%, with persistent effects visible in new construction design choices within five years of settlement.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus contains immigration effects on aggregate house prices and cultural affinity in lending, no study examines how diaspora settlement causally reshapes the hedonic price structure—the implicit valuation of specific architectural attributes—or links cultural demand shifts to new construction design adaptation.
nn_title: Melting pot or salad bowl: Cultural distance and housing investments
assigned_label: Immigration effects on housing markets

research_question: Does the rollout of autonomous vehicle infrastructure—specifically dedicated AV lanes and charging corridors—causally reshape residential and commercial location premiums by decoupling accessibility value from traditional transit proximity?
method: Staggered difference-in-differences exploiting the quasi-random timing of city-level AV infrastructure pilot approvals combined with a hedonic boundary discontinuity design comparing parcels on either side of designated AV corridors.
data: Parcel-level transaction records from ten US pilot cities (2018–2027) merged with FHWA AV infrastructure permit data, Walk Score and transit access indices, and employment center coordinates from the Census Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics dataset.
key_finding: AV corridor proximity is expected to generate positive commercial rent premiums of 5–15% while attenuating traditional rail-station capitalization effects by 20–40%, with heterogeneous effects by land-use density and income level.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus extensively studies rail and highway infrastructure capitalization, no existing work examines how autonomous vehicle infrastructure reshapes the entire accessibility-value mapping, which could fundamentally alter urban rent gradients and submarket definitions.
nn_title: The Impact of Transit Corridors on Residential Property Values
assigned_label: Transportation Infrastructure Impact on Property Values

research_question: How do cognitive load and decision fatigue among mortgage underwriters—induced by processing sequences of loan applications—systematically bias approval and pricing decisions in ways that disadvantage late-in-sequence applicants and minority borrowers?
method: A field experiment exploiting random daily assignment of loan files to underwriters across three large bank servicers, combined with a structural model of sequential decision-making borrowed from behavioral economics to estimate fatigue-induced approval thresholds.
data: Proprietary loan-level underwriting logs from three HMDA-reporting institutions capturing application order, underwriter identity, decision time, approval outcomes, and pricing for approximately 500,000 applications over 2019–2023, linked to borrower demographics.
key_finding: Applicants processed later in an underwriter's daily sequence are expected to face approval rates 3–8 percentage points lower and interest rate spreads 10–20 basis points higher, with disparate impacts concentrated among Black and Hispanic applicants.
novelty_rationale: The corpus treats underwriter discretion and fair lending through static econometric discrimination models; importing sequence-order cognitive fatigue research from behavioral science and judicial decision-making literature to structured mortgage data is entirely absent from existing real estate finance work.
nn_title: How Much Does Racial Bias Affect Mortgage Lending? Evidence from Human and Algor
assigned_label: Mortgage lending: discrimination, pricing, and risk

research_question: Can causal inference on gene-environment interactions—specifically polygenic scores for risk preference and cognitive ability linked to administrative housing registers—quantify the heritable versus environmental determinants of homeownership and mortgage leverage choices?
method: Mendelian randomization using polygenic indices as genetic instruments for risk tolerance and financial sophistication, applied to linked birth-registry, mortgage, and housing transaction data to identify causal pathways net of family wealth and parental homeownership.
data: Nordic population registers (Denmark or Sweden) linking individual genotype arrays from national biobanks to complete mortgage origination and property transaction histories, parental wealth tax records, and neighborhood characteristics for birth cohorts 1960–1980.
key_finding: Genetic predisposition toward risk tolerance is expected to explain 8–15% of variation in leverage choices independently of wealth, while cognitive polygenic scores predict mortgage product sophistication, implying persistent genetic inequality in housing wealth accumulation.
novelty_rationale: The corpus includes one twin-study on homeownership genetics but no work applies polygenic scores and Mendelian randomization—the gold standard in genetic epidemiology—to disentangle heritable from environmental drivers of mortgage and tenure choices at population scale.
nn_title: A Search for the Genetic Contributors to Strategic Mortgage Default: The Catecho
assigned_label: Household Formation, Homeownership, and Social Outcomes

research_question: How do large-scale corporate landlords' rent-setting algorithms—which condition on competitor pricing signals—generate rent co-movement patterns indistinguishable from collusion even absent explicit coordination?
method: A dynamic oligopoly model of algorithmic pricing calibrated to multi-family rental markets, with empirical identification using synthetic control comparisons of rent dispersion and price-cost markups in markets where a common pricing software platform achieves high market penetration versus markets where it does not.
data: RealPage and Yardi market-penetration estimates by metropolitan area merged with CoStar asking-rent panels (2010–2023), American Community Survey rent microdata, and SEC-disclosed software licensing agreements of publicly traded apartment REITs.
key_finding: Algorithmic rent convergence is expected to raise effective rents 3–7% above competitive levels in markets where a single pricing platform covers more than 30% of professionally managed units, with welfare losses concentrated among cost-burdened renters.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus addresses REIT pricing and market power, it contains no work linking industrial organization models of algorithmic collusion to multi-family rental markets, a gap of immediate antitrust and housing-policy significance.
nn_title: Information Asymmetry, Regulations and Equilibrium Outcomes: Theory and Evidence
assigned_label: Rent Control Policy Effects and Outcomes

research_question: Does chronic psychosocial stress from housing insecurity—measured via cortisol assays and self-reported psychological distress in longitudinal health surveys—causally reduce household financial decision quality, creating a behavioral poverty trap that perpetuates rental tenure?
method: Instrumental variables strategy using quasi-random variation in eviction risk exposure (proximity to mass-layoff events interacted with local eviction protections) to instrument for housing insecurity, with financial decision quality measured by mortgage shopping behavior, savings rates, and credit utilization.
data: Panel Study of Income Dynamics biological supplement (cortisol measures) linked to credit bureau records, HMDA mortgage applications, and county-level eviction filing rates from Eviction Lab (2005–2019) for approximately 8,000 households.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation increase in housing insecurity is expected to reduce household savings rates by 2–4 percentage points and increase the probability of high-cost credit use by 15–25%, with effects mediated by elevated cortisol suggesting a stress-cognition pathway.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies housing wealth effects on consumption and homeownership determinants but entirely omits the neuroscience-grounded pathway from housing precarity to financial decision impairment, which bridges social epidemiology and household finance in an unexplored way.
nn_title: Urbanity, Financial Crisis and the Timing of Homebuying Decisions by Young House
assigned_label: Housing Tenure Choice and Transitions

research_question: How does the geographic clustering of patent-intensive innovation activity—revealed through patent citation networks—alter the spatial distribution of commercial real estate risk premia and the information environment for REIT investors?
method: Construction of a time-varying local innovation intensity measure from USPTO patent citation networks at the ZIP-code level, integrated into a Fama-MacBeth cross-sectional asset pricing framework for REIT returns with local beta defined by the REIT's property footprint exposure to innovation clusters.
data: USPTO patent records (1990–2022) geocoded to ZIP codes and aggregated into citation-weighted innovation indices, merged with SNL Real Estate REIT property-level footprint data, CRSP daily REIT returns, and CoStar office vacancy and rent data for 50 major US metros.
key_finding: REITs with higher portfolio exposure to innovation clusters are expected to earn a risk premium of 3–6% annually, with this premium intensifying post-2010 as knowledge-economy agglomeration increased, and weakening during the remote-work shock of 2020–2022.
novelty_rationale: Though the corpus studies agglomeration and local economic effects on REIT returns, using patent citation network topology as a dynamic spatial measure of knowledge-economy risk that is priced in the cross-section of REIT returns is methodologically absent from existing work.
nn_title: The Spatial Equilibrium of Intra-Regional Rates of Return and the Implications f
assigned_label: Real estate portfolio diversification and geographic factors

research_question: Can transfer learning from computer vision models pre-trained on street-level imagery quantify neighborhood physical deterioration trajectories and predict block-level gentrification timing with lead times sufficient for policy intervention?
method: Fine-tuning of a Vision Transformer pre-trained on ImageNet on a labeled dataset of Google Street View images annotated for physical condition indicators, generating annual building-condition embeddings that are input into a survival model of gentrification onset calibrated against repeat-sales price jumps.
data: Google Street View historical imagery (2007–2023) for 50 US cities merged with CoreLogic parcel-level transaction records, Census tract demographics, building permit files, and LIHTC affordable housing locations, covering approximately 20 million property-year observations.
key_finding: Physical deterioration embeddings are expected to predict gentrification onset 3–5 years in advance with AUC exceeding 0.80, substantially outperforming traditional socioeconomic predictors, with false-negative rates higher in majority-Black neighborhoods due to systematic underinvestment.
novelty_rationale: The corpus uses machine learning for automated valuation and hedonic pricing but does not deploy pre-trained vision transformers with transfer learning for neighborhood trajectory prediction, a methodological frontier that could transform early-warning systems for displacement and housing policy targeting.
nn_title: Spatial Prediction of Apartment Rent using Regression-Based and Machine Learning
assigned_label: Machine Learning Automated Property Valuation Models

research_question: How does the legal fiction of corporate personhood in single-asset LLC mortgage structures affect lender recovery rates and loss-given-default relative to direct borrower ownership, and does this vary with judicial foreclosure regimes?
method: A structural model of optimal default and foreclosure timing incorporating LLC pass-through taxation and limited liability features, empirically identified using a regression discontinuity at the threshold of lender-required LLC structuring in commercial mortgage origination policies.
data: CMBS loan-level performance data from Trepp (1998–2023) with borrower entity type (LLC vs. individual vs. corporate), loan origination characteristics, state foreclosure law classifications, liquidation outcomes, and appraised recovery values for approximately 150,000 loans.
key_finding: LLC-structured loans are expected to show loss-given-default rates 8–15 percentage points higher than direct borrower loans in judicial foreclosure states due to strategic default facilitation, while showing no significant difference in non-judicial states, implying a systematic mispricing of legal structure risk in CMBS spreads.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies recourse versus nonrecourse lending and foreclosure law effects separately but ignores the intersection of LLC legal entity structure—now dominant in commercial real estate—with optimal default theory and lender recovery, a gap with substantial commercial mortgage pricing implications.
nn_title: Originator Performance, CMBS Structures, and the Risk of Commercial Mortgages
assigned_label: Mortgage behavior, defaults, and termination risks

research_question: Does ambient noise pollution from urban freight logistics networks—quantified through acoustic sensor arrays—create previously unmeasured discount gradients in industrial and residential property values that are systematically omitted from standard hedonic models?
method: Difference-in-differences exploiting the staggered deployment of quiet-zone mandates under FRA regulations at rail crossings and electric vehicle fleet mandates for delivery services, with hedonic models augmented by interpolated continuous noise maps from city acoustic sensor networks.
data: City acoustic sensor network data from New York, Chicago, and Los Angeles (2015–2023) merged with parcel-level transaction records from county assessors, freight logistics network maps from the Freight Analysis Framework, and FRA quiet-zone compliance records.
key_finding: A 10-decibel reduction in freight-origin noise is expected to increase residential property values by 2–5% and industrial property values by 1–3%, with effects concentrated within 200 meters of major logistics corridors and larger for nighttime noise, revealing systematic under-capitalization of logistics externalities in standard hedonic estimates.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus studies traffic noise and airport proximity, no work uses continuous acoustic sensor array data to isolate freight logistics noise as a distinct externality source with time-of-day variation, an omission increasingly important as e-commerce reshapes urban freight patterns.
nn_title: Railway sound barriers and housing prices
assigned_label: Transportation Infrastructure Impact on Property Values

research_question: How does soil microbiome diversity and urban ecological health—detectable from satellite-derived vegetation spectral indices and field sampling—capitalize into residential property values and influence the spatial incidence of greenspace premiums across income and racial groups?
method: Instrumental variables hedonic analysis using quasi-random variation in historical industrial contamination remediation timelines to identify the causal effect of improved soil and vegetation ecological quality on property values, with heterogeneity analysis across income and race using Census microdata.
data: EPA Superfund and brownfield remediation records linked to USGS satellite vegetation spectral data (NDVI, soil moisture indices) at 30-meter resolution (2000–2023), CoreLogic parcel transaction data for 30 US cities, and ACS demographic records, covering approximately 5 million transactions.
key_finding: A one-standard-deviation improvement in ecological soil health is expected to generate property value premiums of 3–8%, concentrated in high-income neighborhoods, while low-income and minority neighborhoods receive 40–60% smaller premiums for equivalent ecological improvements, suggesting spatial equity failures in greenspace investment.
novelty_rationale: The corpus studies greenspace proximity and open space amenities using conventional distance metrics but entirely overlooks ecological quality heterogeneity within greenspaces—importing environmental science's soil and vegetation health measures into hedonic modeling is a methodologically novel contribution that bridges ecology and real estate economics.
nn_title: A Meta - Analysis of the Effect of Environmental Contamination and Positive Amen
assigned_label: Environmental Contamination and Property Values

research_question: Do large language model embeddings of lease contract text capture latent clauses—exclusivity provisions, co-tenancy requirements, radius restrictions—that predict commercial property default and cap rate compression beyond standard financial underwriting variables?
method: Natural language processing using pre-trained LLM embeddings applied to a corpus of scraped and parsed commercial lease agreements, with embeddings entered as high-dimensional features in gradient-boosted default hazard models and cap rate regressions alongside standard controls, with Shapley value decomposition to identify which contractual dimensions drive predictions.
data: Proprietary CMBS loan-level data linked to underlying lease abstracts from CoStar and MSCI/NCREIF, covering approximately 50,000 commercial leases across office, retail, and industrial properties from 2000–2023.
key_finding: Lease contract complexity measures—particularly co-tenancy and exclusivity clause density—are expected to explain 10–20% incremental variance in default risk and cap rate spreads beyond LTV, DSCR, and property type, with retail properties showing the strongest effects as anchor co-tenancy triggers materialize post-COVID.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus contains extensive CMBS default and cap rate research and nascent text-mining studies, no paper extracts structural legal contract features from lease text as predictors of mortgage or investment performance, importing NLP contract analysis from law-and-finance into real estate credit risk.
nn_title: On the Relationship between Market Sentiment and Commercial Real Estate Performa
assigned_label: Real Estate Market Liquidity and Efficiency

research_question: Does the spatial concentration of maternal healthcare deserts—areas with inadequate obstetric and prenatal services—causally reduce residential property values and accelerate neighborhood decline beyond what income, crime, and school quality measures capture?
method: A difference-in-differences design exploiting staggered closures of hospital obstetric units as plausibly exogenous shocks to local healthcare access, with hedonic repeat-sales regressions on geocoded residential transactions within concentric rings around affected facilities, instrumenting for unit closure timing using state certificate-of-need law changes.
data: American Hospital Association annual surveys identifying obstetric unit openings and closures from 2000–2022, merged with CoreLogic residential transaction records and American Community Survey neighborhood covariates at the census tract level.
key_finding: Obstetric unit closures are expected to reduce nearby home values by 3–8% within a two-mile radius, with larger effects in rural counties and majority-minority neighborhoods, suggesting healthcare infrastructure is a previously underappreciated driver of residential capitalization beyond the school quality and crime channels extensively studied in the corpus.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains extensive work on healthcare facility proximity (medical office rents) and neighborhood amenity capitalization but entirely omits healthcare desert formation as an endogenous driver of housing values, importing a causal healthcare access framework from health economics into hedonic pricing.
nn_title: JUE Insight: Difference-in-differences with geocoded microdata
assigned_label: Spatial econometrics in real estate valuation

research_question: Does the institutional structure of mortgage servicer compensation—specifically the mismatch between fixed per-loan servicing fees and variable workout costs—systematically distort loss mitigation decisions in ways that can be identified through a structural model of servicer optimization?
method: A dynamic discrete-choice structural model of servicer loss mitigation decisions—modification, short sale, deed-in-lieu, foreclosure—estimated using HAMP/Making Home Affordable loan-level administrative data, with servicer fixed effects and quasi-experimental variation from HAMP incentive payment schedule changes used to identify the compensation elasticity of workout choice.
data: Treasury HAMP administrative data covering approximately 1.7 million modification applications from 2009–2016, linked to Black Knight mortgage performance data tracking post-modification outcomes, with servicer identity allowing for firm-level compensation structure variation.
key_finding: Servicers facing higher fixed per-loan costs relative to modification incentive payments are expected to systematically under-modify loans that would pass a net-present-value test by 15–25%, with compensation structure explaining a larger share of modification propensity variation than borrower credit risk, revealing a structural inefficiency in the post-crisis loss mitigation architecture.
novelty_rationale: The corpus has substantial servicer incentive research but largely uses reduced-form approaches; this structural identification of the compensation-behavior elasticity using policy variation in HAMP payment schedules represents a methodological advance that could generalize to designing optimal servicer contracts.
nn_title: Servicers and Mortgage‐Backed Securities Default: Theory and Evidence
assigned_label: Mortgage behavior, defaults, and termination risks

research_question: How does the spatial allocation of federal Opportunity Zone tax incentives interact with pre-existing real estate market liquidity to produce heterogeneous capitalization effects, and can a general equilibrium sorting model explain why benefits flow to developers rather than incumbent residents?
method: A quantitative spatial equilibrium model with heterogeneous households, landlords, and developers calibrated to Census tract-level data, with the model's predictions tested against a regression discontinuity design exploiting the income eligibility threshold for Opportunity Zone designation, augmented with individual-level migration data from the IRS Statistics of Income to track resident displacement.
data: IRS Opportunity Zone designation boundaries linked to CoStar commercial transaction records, Zillow residential price and rent indices, IRS migration statistics, and American Community Survey tract-level demographics from 2015–2023 for approximately 8,760 designated and adjacent non-designated tracts.
key_finding: The model predicts and the data are expected to confirm that capitalization of Opportunity Zone benefits into land values is 60–80% complete within three years in high-liquidity markets, while low-liquidity designated tracts show minimal investment response, with IRS migration data revealing net displacement of low-income residents in high-liquidity zones, suggesting the program's incidence is fundamentally shaped by pre-existing market structure.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus includes Opportunity Zone studies using difference-in-differences, none combines a structural sorting model with granular IRS displacement tracking to characterize the program's welfare incidence across market liquidity regimes, importing quantitative spatial economics methodology from urban economics into real estate policy analysis.
nn_title: The Spatial Distribution of Housing‐Related Ordinary Income Tax Benefits
assigned_label: Property taxation effects on housing markets

research_question: Can satellite-derived nighttime light intensity and daytime spectral signatures—as continuous proxies for economic activity, construction, and land use change—be used to construct real-time commercial real estate performance indicators in markets where transaction-based indices are unavailable or severely lagged?
method: Machine learning prediction models mapping VIIRS nighttime light radiance, Sentinel-2 multispectral indices, and Google Earth Engine change detection outputs to quarterly NCREIF property-level returns and NPI sub-index returns, estimated on a training set of US markets with rich transaction data and then transferred to predict performance in data-sparse international and secondary markets.
data: NASA VIIRS nighttime light data and ESA Sentinel-2 imagery at 10-meter resolution from 2012–2023 for 150 global metropolitan areas, linked to NCREIF NPI property-level returns for US markets and MSCI/IPD international indices for out-of-sample validation in 40 countries.
key_finding: Satellite-derived indicators are expected to predict quarterly commercial real estate total returns with an out-of-sample R² of 0.30–0.50 in US markets and 0.20–0.35 in international secondary markets, with nighttime light growth rate changes leading transaction-based index revisions by one to two quarters, enabling near-real-time performance monitoring in markets where institutional investment occurs but index coverage is minimal.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains no work using remote sensing imagery as a performance measurement tool for private commercial real estate, importing geospatial Earth observation methods from development economics and land use science into institutional real estate index construction.
nn_title: A Machine Learning Approach to Price Indices: Applications in Commercial Real Es
assigned_label: Real estate price index methodology and measurement

research_question: Does the algorithmic coordination of short-term rental pricing across platforms—through shared revenue management software that serves both Airbnb and long-term rental operators—function as a form of tacit collusion that raises equilibrium rents in local housing markets beyond what competitive platforms would produce?
method: A structural oligopolistic competition model of rental housing supply estimated using variation in revenue management software market penetration across metropolitan areas, identified through an instrumental variables strategy based on the geographic rollout of software vendor data center infrastructure, with outcomes measured on Zillow Observed Rent Index and CoStar multifamily vacancy data.
data: Airbnb and VRBO listing data with revenue management software identifiers scraped from public APIs for 100 US metropolitan areas from 2015–2023, linked to Zillow Observed Rent Index, CoStar multifamily fundamentals, and AirDNA occupancy and pricing data for approximately 10 million short-term rental listings.
key_finding: Metropolitan areas where more than 30% of rental units use shared revenue management platforms are expected to show 4–9% higher equilibrium long-term rents relative to competitive counterfactuals, with the effect concentrated in supply-constrained coastal markets, suggesting that software-mediated algorithmic coordination constitutes a previously unrecognized mechanism linking short-term rental platforms to housing affordability beyond the direct unit conversion channel studied in the corpus.
novelty_rationale: The corpus contains Airbnb regulation studies measuring unit conversion effects but none examines pricing algorithm coordination as a collusion-adjacent mechanism raising equilibrium rents, importing industrial organization methods from antitrust economics into housing market competition analysis.
nn_title: How do short‐term rental regulations affect market outcomes? Evidence from San F
assigned_label: Rent Control Policy Effects and Outcomes

research_question: Do persistent intergenerational wealth gaps in homeownership stem more from differential access to parental co-signing of mortgages—which circumvents credit score thresholds—than from down payment transfers, and does co-signer availability explain racial homeownership gaps conditional on income and credit history?
method: A regression discontinuity design exploiting FHFA conforming loan credit score cutoffs that require co-signers for borderline applicants, combined with linked parent-child credit bureau data to measure parental credit quality availability, instrumenting for co-signer access using variation in state filial financial responsibility laws that affect parental credit risk and willingness to co-sign.
data: Linked parent-child credit bureau panel data from Federal Reserve Bank of New York/Equifax Consumer Credit Panel matched to HMDA mortgage origination records from 2005–2022, with state filial law variation as an instrument, covering approximately 2 million first-time homebuyer observations.
key_finding: Access to a credit-eligible co-signer is expected to increase first-time homebuyer mortgage approval probability by 18–30% for borderline applicants, with Black and Hispanic applicants facing a 40–55% lower probability of having a credit-eligible parent available as co-signer compared to white applicants with identical incomes and credit scores, explaining a larger fraction of the racial homeownership gap than down payment availability alone.
novelty_rationale: While the corpus contains extensive research on racial homeownership gaps, down payments, and parental wealth transfers, no paper examines parental co-signing capacity as a distinct credit access mechanism using linked parent-child credit bureau data with filial law instruments, combining family economics with mortgage credit rationing research.
nn_title: Differential Access to and Pricing of Home Mortgages: 2004 through 2009
assigned_label: Mortgage lending: discrimination, pricing, and risk

research_question: Does the mandatory adoption of building-level energy performance certificates (EPCs) create information cascades in residential mortgage underwriting—whereby lenders update collateral valuations based on energy ratings—that ultimately recapitalize energy efficiency into mortgage-implied property values faster than arm's-length market transactions?
method: A structural learning model of lender collateral valuation calibrated to estimate the speed at which EPC disclosure shifts automated valuation model predictions, identified through a staggered difference-in-differences design exploiting the phased mandatory disclosure rollout across EU member states, with mortgage-implied values backed out from loan-to-value ratios and loan amounts as the primary outcome.
data: EU-harmonized EPC register data linked to mortgage origination records from six European countries (Netherlands, Germany, France, Belgium, Sweden, Denmark) covering approximately 8 million transactions from 2008–2022, with EPC mandatory disclosure dates varying by country and property vintage.
